search_text: Scaling Up Dynamic Graph Representation Learning via Spiking Neural Networks 通过尖峰神经网络扩展动态图表示学习 Recent years have seen a surge in research on dynamic graph representation learning, which aims to model temporal graphs that are dynamic and evolving constantly over time. However, current work typically models graph dynamics with recurrent neural networks (RNNs), making them suffer seriously from computation and memory overheads on large temporal graphs. So far, scalability of dynamic graph representation learning on large temporal graphs remains one of the major challenges. In this paper, we present a scalable framework, namely SpikeNet, to efficiently capture the temporal and structural patterns of temporal graphs. We explore a new direction in that we can capture the evolving dynamics of temporal graphs with spiking neural networks (SNNs) instead of RNNs. As a low-power alternative to RNNs, SNNs explicitly model graph dynamics as spike trains of neuron populations and enable spike-based propagation in an efficient way. Experiments on three large real-world temporal graph datasets demonstrate that SpikeNet outperforms strong baselines on the temporal node classification task with lower computational costs. Particularly, SpikeNet generalizes to a large temporal graph (2.7M nodes and 13.9M edges) with significantly fewer parameters and computation overheads.Our code is publicly available at \url{https://github.com/EdisonLeeeee/SpikeNet}. 近年来，动态图表示学习的研究激增，旨在对动态且随时间不断变化的时间图进行建模。然而，当前的工作通常使用循环神经网络（RNN）对图动态进行建模，这使得它们严重受到大型时序图的计算和内存开销的影响。到目前为止，大型时序图上动态图表示学习的可扩展性仍然是主要挑战之一。在本文中，我们提出了一个可扩展的框架，即 SpikeNet，以有效捕获时间图的时间和结构模式。我们探索了一个新的方向，即我们可以使用尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 而不是 RNN 来捕获时间图的动态演化。作为 RNN 的低功耗替代方案，SNN 将图动态显式建模为神经元群体的尖峰序列，并以有效的方式实现基于尖峰的传播。对三个大型现实世界时间图数据集的实验表明，SpikeNet 在时间节点分类任务上优于强基线，且计算成本较低。特别是，SpikeNet 可以推广到一个大型时间图（270 万个节点和 1390 万条边），参数和计算开销显着减少。我们的代码可在 \url{https://github.com/EdisonLeeeee/SpikeNet} 上公开获取。 本文提出一种基于脉冲神经网络的可扩展动态图表示学习框架SpikeNet，以解决传统方法在大规模动态图上的计算和内存瓶颈问题。 Jintang Li,Zhouxin Yu,Zulun Zhu,Liang Chen,Qi Yu,Zibin Zheng,Sheng Tian,Ruofan Wu,Changhua Meng cs.NE,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Jingxi Li,Bijie Bai,Yi Luo,Aydogan Ozcan
title: Massively Parallel Universal Linear Transformations using a Wavelength-Multiplexed Diffractive Optical Network
title_zh: 使用波长复用衍射光网络的大规模并行通用线性变换
abstract: We report deep learning-based design of a massively parallel broadband diffractive neural network for all-optically performing a large group of arbitrarily-selected, complex-valued linear transformations between an input and output field-of-view, each with N_i and N_o pixels, respectively. This broadband diffractive processor is composed of N_w wavelength channels, each of which is uniquely assigned to a distinct target transformation. A large set of arbitrarily-selected linear transformations can be individually performed through the same diffractive network at different illumination wavelengths, either simultaneously or sequentially (wavelength scanning). We demonstrate that such a broadband diffractive network, regardless of its material dispersion, can successfully approximate N_w unique complex-valued linear transforms with a negligible error when the number of diffractive neurons (N) in its design matches or exceeds 2 x N_w x N_i x N_o. We further report that the spectral multiplexing capability (N_w) can be increased by increasing N; our numerical analyses confirm these conclusions for N_w > 180, which can be further increased to e.g., ~2000 depending on the upper bound of the approximation error. Massively parallel, wavelength-multiplexed diffractive networks will be useful for designing high-throughput intelligent machine vision systems and hyperspectral processors that can perform statistical inference and analyze objects/scenes with unique spectral properties.
summary: We report deep learning-based design of a massively parallel broadband diffractive neural network for all-optically performing a large group of arbitrarily-selected, complex-valued linear transformations between an input and output field-of-view, each with N_i and N_o pixels, respectively. This broadband diffractive processor is composed of N_w wavelength channels, each of which is uniquely assigned to a distinct target transformation. A large set of arbitrarily-selected linear transformations can be individually performed through the same diffractive network at different illumination wavelengths, either simultaneously or sequentially (wavelength scanning). We demonstrate that such a broadband diffractive network, regardless of its material dispersion, can successfully approximate N_w unique complex-valued linear transforms with a negligible error when the number of diffractive neurons (N) in its design matches or exceeds 2 x N_w x N_i x N_o. We further report that the spectral multiplexing capability (N_w) can be increased by increasing N; our numerical analyses confirm these conclusions for N_w > 180, which can be further increased to e.g., ~2000 depending on the upper bound of the approximation error. Massively parallel, wavelength-multiplexed diffractive networks will be useful for designing high-throughput intelligent machine vision systems and hyperspectral processors that can perform statistical inference and analyze objects/scenes with unique spectral properties.
abstract_zh: 我们报告了基于深度学习的大规模并行宽带衍射神经网络的设计，用于全光学地在输入和输出视场之间执行一大组任意选择的复值线性变换，每个变换分别具有 N_i 和 N_o 像素。该宽带衍射处理器由 N_w 个波长通道组成，每个通道都唯一分配给不同的目标变换。可以通过同一衍射网络在不同的照明波长下同时或顺序（波长扫描）单独执行大量任意选择的线性变换。我们证明，当设计中的衍射神经元数量 (N) 匹配或超过 2 x N_w x N_i x N_o 时，这种宽带衍射网络，无论其材料色散如何，都可以成功地近似 N_w 独特的复值线性变换，并且误差可以忽略不计。我们进一步报告说，可以通过增加N来增加频谱复用能力（N_w）；我们的数值分析证实了 N_w > 180 时的这些结论，根据近似误差的上限，可以进一步增加到例如 ~2000。大规模并行、波长复用衍射网络将有助于设计高通量智能机器视觉系统和高光谱处理器，这些系统和高光谱处理器可以执行统计推断并分析具有独特光谱特性的物体/场景。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于深度学习设计的宽带衍射神经网络，能够通过不同波长的光同时或依次执行大量任意选择的复值线性变换。
AI.motivation: 开发一种高通量光学处理器，以实现机器视觉和高光谱分析中对多组线性变换的并行处理需求。
AI.method: 利用深度学习设计包含N_w个波长通道的衍射网络，每个通道对应特定变换；通过增加衍射神经元数量（N≥2×N_w×N_i×N_o）提升并行处理能力，并通过数值分析验证性能。
AI.result: 实验表明，当N足够大时，网络可近似实现N_w=180以上的独立线性变换，且误差可控制在可接受范围，理论上N_w可扩展至约2000。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为高通量智能视觉系统和高光谱处理器提供了新型光学架构，支持复杂场景的光谱特性分析与统计推理。

authors_text: Zhanyu Wang,Mingkang Tang,Lei Wang,Xiu Li,Luping Zhou
title: A Medical Semantic-Assisted Transformer for Radiographic Report Generation
title_zh: 用于生成射线报告的医学语义辅助变压器
abstract: Automated radiographic report generation is a challenging cross-domain task that aims to automatically generate accurate and semantic-coherence reports to describe medical images. Despite the recent progress in this field, there are still many challenges at least in the following aspects. First, radiographic images are very similar to each other, and thus it is difficult to capture the fine-grained visual differences using CNN as the visual feature extractor like many existing methods. Further, semantic information has been widely applied to boost the performance of generation tasks (e.g. image captioning), but existing methods often fail to provide effective medical semantic features. Toward solving those problems, in this paper, we propose a memory-augmented sparse attention block utilizing bilinear pooling to capture the higher-order interactions between the input fine-grained image features while producing sparse attention. Moreover, we introduce a novel Medical Concepts Generation Network (MCGN) to predict fine-grained semantic concepts and incorporate them into the report generation process as guidance. Our proposed method shows promising performance on the recently released largest benchmark MIMIC-CXR. It outperforms multiple state-of-the-art methods in image captioning and medical report generation.
summary: Automated radiographic report generation is a challenging cross-domain task that aims to automatically generate accurate and semantic-coherence reports to describe medical images. Despite the recent progress in this field, there are still many challenges at least in the following aspects. First, radiographic images are very similar to each other, and thus it is difficult to capture the fine-grained visual differences using CNN as the visual feature extractor like many existing methods. Further, semantic information has been widely applied to boost the performance of generation tasks (e.g. image captioning), but existing methods often fail to provide effective medical semantic features. Toward solving those problems, in this paper, we propose a memory-augmented sparse attention block utilizing bilinear pooling to capture the higher-order interactions between the input fine-grained image features while producing sparse attention. Moreover, we introduce a novel Medical Concepts Generation Network (MCGN) to predict fine-grained semantic concepts and incorporate them into the report generation process as guidance. Our proposed method shows promising performance on the recently released largest benchmark MIMIC-CXR. It outperforms multiple state-of-the-art methods in image captioning and medical report generation.
abstract_zh: 自动射线照相报告生成是一项具有挑战性的跨领域任务，旨在自动生成准确且语义一致的报告来描述医学图像。尽管该领域最近取得了进展，但至少在以下方面仍然存在许多挑战。首先，放射线图像彼此非常相似，因此很难像许多现有方法一样使用 CNN 作为视觉特征提取器来捕获细粒度的视觉差异。此外，语义信息已被广泛应用于提高生成任务（例如图像字幕）的性能，但现有方法往往无法提供有效的医学语义特征。为了解决这些问题，在本文中，我们提出了一种内存增强的稀疏注意力块，利用双线性池来捕获输入细粒度图像特征之间的高阶交互，同时产生稀疏注意力。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的医学概念生成网络（MCGN）来预测细粒度语义概念并将其作为指导纳入报告生成过程中。我们提出的方法在最近发布的最大基准 MIMIC-CXR 上显示出良好的性能。它在图像字幕和医疗报告生成方面优于多种最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合医学语义的Transformer模型，用于生成语义连贯的放射科影像报告。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以捕捉放射影像的细微视觉差异，且缺乏有效的医学语义特征来提升生成效果。
AI.method: 设计记忆增强的稀疏注意力模块（含双线性池化）捕捉高阶特征交互，并引入医学概念生成网络（MCGN）预测细粒度语义概念指导报告生成。
AI.result: 在MIMIC-CXR基准测试中优于多种先进方法，在影像描述和医学报告生成任务中表现突出。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合稀疏注意力机制与医学语义概念，有效提升了放射报告生成的准确性与语义连贯性。

search_text: A Medical Semantic-Assisted Transformer for Radiographic Report Generation 用于生成射线报告的医学语义辅助变压器 Automated radiographic report generation is a challenging cross-domain task that aims to automatically generate accurate and semantic-coherence reports to describe medical images. Despite the recent progress in this field, there are still many challenges at least in the following aspects. First, radiographic images are very similar to each other, and thus it is difficult to capture the fine-grained visual differences using CNN as the visual feature extractor like many existing methods. Further, semantic information has been widely applied to boost the performance of generation tasks (e.g. image captioning), but existing methods often fail to provide effective medical semantic features. Toward solving those problems, in this paper, we propose a memory-augmented sparse attention block utilizing bilinear pooling to capture the higher-order interactions between the input fine-grained image features while producing sparse attention. Moreover, we introduce a novel Medical Concepts Generation Network (MCGN) to predict fine-grained semantic concepts and incorporate them into the report generation process as guidance. Our proposed method shows promising performance on the recently released largest benchmark MIMIC-CXR. It outperforms multiple state-of-the-art methods in image captioning and medical report generation. 自动射线照相报告生成是一项具有挑战性的跨领域任务，旨在自动生成准确且语义一致的报告来描述医学图像。尽管该领域最近取得了进展，但至少在以下方面仍然存在许多挑战。首先，放射线图像彼此非常相似，因此很难像许多现有方法一样使用 CNN 作为视觉特征提取器来捕获细粒度的视觉差异。此外，语义信息已被广泛应用于提高生成任务（例如图像字幕）的性能，但现有方法往往无法提供有效的医学语义特征。为了解决这些问题，在本文中，我们提出了一种内存增强的稀疏注意力块，利用双线性池来捕获输入细粒度图像特征之间的高阶交互，同时产生稀疏注意力。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的医学概念生成网络（MCGN）来预测细粒度语义概念并将其作为指导纳入报告生成过程中。我们提出的方法在最近发布的最大基准 MIMIC-CXR 上显示出良好的性能。它在图像字幕和医疗报告生成方面优于多种最先进的方法。 本文提出一种结合医学语义的Transformer模型，用于生成语义连贯的放射科影像报告。 Zhanyu Wang,Mingkang Tang,Lei Wang,Xiu Li,Luping Zhou cs.CV

authors_text: Adnen Abdessaied,Mihai Bâce,Andreas Bulling
title: Neuro-Symbolic Visual Dialog
title_zh: 神经符号视觉对话
abstract: We propose Neuro-Symbolic Visual Dialog (NSVD) -the first method to combine deep learning and symbolic program execution for multi-round visually-grounded reasoning. NSVD significantly outperforms existing purely-connectionist methods on two key challenges inherent to visual dialog: long-distance co-reference resolution as well as vanishing question-answering performance. We demonstrate the latter by proposing a more realistic and stricter evaluation scheme in which we use predicted answers for the full dialog history when calculating accuracy. We describe two variants of our model and show that using this new scheme, our best model achieves an accuracy of 99.72% on CLEVR-Dialog -a relative improvement of more than 10% over the state of the art while only requiring a fraction of training data. Moreover, we demonstrate that our neuro-symbolic models have a higher mean first failure round, are more robust against incomplete dialog histories, and generalise better not only to dialogs that are up to three times longer than those seen during training but also to unseen question types and scenes.
summary: We propose Neuro-Symbolic Visual Dialog (NSVD) -the first method to combine deep learning and symbolic program execution for multi-round visually-grounded reasoning. NSVD significantly outperforms existing purely-connectionist methods on two key challenges inherent to visual dialog: long-distance co-reference resolution as well as vanishing question-answering performance. We demonstrate the latter by proposing a more realistic and stricter evaluation scheme in which we use predicted answers for the full dialog history when calculating accuracy. We describe two variants of our model and show that using this new scheme, our best model achieves an accuracy of 99.72% on CLEVR-Dialog -a relative improvement of more than 10% over the state of the art while only requiring a fraction of training data. Moreover, we demonstrate that our neuro-symbolic models have a higher mean first failure round, are more robust against incomplete dialog histories, and generalise better not only to dialogs that are up to three times longer than those seen during training but also to unseen question types and scenes.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了神经符号视觉对话（NSVD）——第一种将深度学习和符号程序执行相结合进行多轮视觉推理的方法。 NSVD 在视觉对话固有的两个关键挑战上显着优于现有的纯联结主义方法：长距离共指解析以及消失的问答性能。我们通过提出一种更现实、更严格的评估方案来证明后者，在该方案中，我们在计算准确性时使用完整对话历史的预测答案。我们描述了模型的两种变体，并表明，使用这种新方案，我们的最佳模型在 CLEVR-Dialog 上的准确率达到了 99.72%，与现有技术相比相对提高了 10% 以上，同时只需要一小部分训练数据。此外，我们证明我们的神经符号模型具有更高的平均首轮失败率，对不完整的对话历史更加稳健，并且不仅能够更好地泛化到比训练期间看到的对话长三倍的对话，而且还能够更好地泛化到未见过的问题类型和场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合深度学习与符号程序执行的神经符号视觉对话方法（NSVD），用于解决多轮视觉对话中的长期共指解析及问答性能下降问题。
AI.motivation: 现有纯连接主义方法在视觉对话任务中存在长距离共指解析困难及问答性能随对话轮次增加而衰减的缺陷，需更鲁棒的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计NSVD框架融合神经网络与符号推理，提出两种模型变体，并采用基于完整对话历史预测答案的严格评估方案。
AI.result: 在CLEVR-Dialog数据集上达到99.72%准确率（较SOTA提升超10%），仅需少量训练数据，且模型对长对话、新问题类型及不完整对话历史更具鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 神经符号方法在视觉对话任务中展现出更优的推理能力与泛化性，为复杂多轮对话系统提供了新方向。

authors_text: Hanting Li,Hongjing Niu,Zhaoqing Zhu,Feng Zhao
title: Intensity-Aware Loss for Dynamic Facial Expression Recognition in the Wild
title_zh: 野外动态面部表情识别的强度感知损失
abstract: Compared with the image-based static facial expression recognition (SFER) task, the dynamic facial expression recognition (DFER) task based on video sequences is closer to the natural expression recognition scene. However, DFER is often more challenging. One of the main reasons is that video sequences often contain frames with different expression intensities, especially for the facial expressions in the real-world scenarios, while the images in SFER frequently present uniform and high expression intensities. However, if the expressions with different intensities are treated equally, the features learned by the networks will have large intra-class and small inter-class differences, which is harmful to DFER. To tackle this problem, we propose the global convolution-attention block (GCA) to rescale the channels of the feature maps. In addition, we introduce the intensity-aware loss (IAL) in the training process to help the network distinguish the samples with relatively low expression intensities. Experiments on two in-the-wild dynamic facial expression datasets (i.e., DFEW and FERV39k) indicate that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art DFER approaches. The source code will be made publicly available.
summary: Compared with the image-based static facial expression recognition (SFER) task, the dynamic facial expression recognition (DFER) task based on video sequences is closer to the natural expression recognition scene. However, DFER is often more challenging. One of the main reasons is that video sequences often contain frames with different expression intensities, especially for the facial expressions in the real-world scenarios, while the images in SFER frequently present uniform and high expression intensities. However, if the expressions with different intensities are treated equally, the features learned by the networks will have large intra-class and small inter-class differences, which is harmful to DFER. To tackle this problem, we propose the global convolution-attention block (GCA) to rescale the channels of the feature maps. In addition, we introduce the intensity-aware loss (IAL) in the training process to help the network distinguish the samples with relatively low expression intensities. Experiments on two in-the-wild dynamic facial expression datasets (i.e., DFEW and FERV39k) indicate that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art DFER approaches. The source code will be made publicly available.
abstract_zh: 与基于图像的静态面部表情识别（SFER）任务相比，基于视频序列的动态面部表情识别（DFER）任务更接近自然表情识别场景。然而，DFER 通常更具挑战性。主要原因之一是视频序列通常包含不同表情强度的帧，尤其是现实场景中的面部表情，而SFER中的图像经常呈现均匀且高的表情强度。然而，如果对不同强度的表达进行同等对待，网络学习到的特征将具有较大的类内差异和较小的类间差异，这对DFER是有害的。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了全局卷积注意块（GCA）来重新调整特征图的通道。此外，我们在训练过程中引入强度感知损失（IAL）来帮助网络区分表达强度相对较低的样本。对两个野外动态面部表情数据集（即 DFEW 和 FERV39k）的实验表明，我们的方法优于最先进的 DFER 方法。源代码将公开。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种强度感知损失（IAL）和全局卷积注意力块（GCA），以解决动态面部表情识别（DFER）中因表情强度差异导致的特征学习问题。
AI.motivation: 动态面部表情识别（DFER）在真实场景中面临表情强度不一致的挑战，现有方法对不同强度表情处理方式相同，导致类内差异大、类间差异小，影响识别效果。
AI.method: 设计全局卷积-注意力块（GCA）对特征图通道进行重缩放，并引入强度感知损失（IAL）在训练过程中增强对低强度表情样本的区分能力。
AI.result: 在DFEW和FERV39k两个真实场景数据集上，该方法优于当前最先进的DFER方法，验证了有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过结合GCA和IAL，本文有效解决了DFER中表情强度差异带来的挑战，为动态表情识别提供了新思路，代码将开源。

In addition, we introduce the intensity-aware loss (IAL) in the training process to help the network distinguish the samples with relatively low expression intensities. Experiments on two in-the-wild dynamic facial expression datasets (i.e., DFEW and FERV39k) indicate that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art DFER approaches. The source code will be made publicly available. 与基于图像的静态面部表情识别（SFER）任务相比，基于视频序列的动态面部表情识别（DFER）任务更接近自然表情识别场景。然而，DFER 通常更具挑战性。主要原因之一是视频序列通常包含不同表情强度的帧，尤其是现实场景中的面部表情，而SFER中的图像经常呈现均匀且高的表情强度。然而，如果对不同强度的表达进行同等对待，网络学习到的特征将具有较大的类内差异和较小的类间差异，这对DFER是有害的。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了全局卷积注意块（GCA）来重新调整特征图的通道。此外，我们在训练过程中引入强度感知损失（IAL）来帮助网络区分表达强度相对较低的样本。对两个野外动态面部表情数据集（即 DFEW 和 FERV39k）的实验表明，我们的方法优于最先进的 DFER 方法。源代码将公开。 本文提出一种强度感知损失（IAL）和全局卷积注意力块（GCA），以解决动态面部表情识别（DFER）中因表情强度差异导致的特征学习问题。 Hanting Li,Hongjing Niu,Zhaoqing Zhu,Feng Zhao cs.CV

authors_text: Alexander Kalinowski,Yuan An
title: Repurposing Knowledge Graph Embeddings for Triple Representation via Weak Supervision
title_zh: 通过弱监督重新利用知识图嵌入以实现三重表示
abstract: The majority of knowledge graph embedding techniques treat entities and predicates as separate embedding matrices, using aggregation functions to build a representation of the input triple. However, these aggregations are lossy, i.e. they do not capture the semantics of the original triples, such as information contained in the predicates. To combat these shortcomings, current methods learn triple embeddings from scratch without utilizing entity and predicate embeddings from pre-trained models. In this paper, we design a novel fine-tuning approach for learning triple embeddings by creating weak supervision signals from pre-trained knowledge graph embeddings. We develop a method for automatically sampling triples from a knowledge graph and estimating their pairwise similarities from pre-trained embedding models. These pairwise similarity scores are then fed to a Siamese-like neural architecture to fine-tune triple representations. We evaluate the proposed method on two widely studied knowledge graphs and show consistent improvement over other state-of-the-art triple embedding methods on triple classification and triple clustering tasks.
summary: The majority of knowledge graph embedding techniques treat entities and predicates as separate embedding matrices, using aggregation functions to build a representation of the input triple. However, these aggregations are lossy, i.e. they do not capture the semantics of the original triples, such as information contained in the predicates. To combat these shortcomings, current methods learn triple embeddings from scratch without utilizing entity and predicate embeddings from pre-trained models. In this paper, we design a novel fine-tuning approach for learning triple embeddings by creating weak supervision signals from pre-trained knowledge graph embeddings. We develop a method for automatically sampling triples from a knowledge graph and estimating their pairwise similarities from pre-trained embedding models. These pairwise similarity scores are then fed to a Siamese-like neural architecture to fine-tune triple representations. We evaluate the proposed method on two widely studied knowledge graphs and show consistent improvement over other state-of-the-art triple embedding methods on triple classification and triple clustering tasks.
abstract_zh: 大多数知识图嵌入技术将实体和谓词视为单独的嵌入矩阵，使用聚合函数构建输入三元组的表示。然而，这些聚合是有损的，即它们不捕获原始三元组的语义，例如谓词中包含的信息。为了克服这些缺点，当前的方法从头开始学习三重嵌入，而不利用预训练模型中的实体和谓词嵌入。在本文中，我们设计了一种新颖的微调方法，通过从预先训练的知识图嵌入创建弱监督信号来学习三重嵌入。我们开发了一种从知识图中自动采样三元组并根据预训练的嵌入模型估计它们的成对相似性的方法。然后，这些成对的相似性分数被输入到类似 Siamese 的神经架构中，以微调三重表示。我们在两个广泛研究的知识图上评估了所提出的方法，并在三重分类和三重聚类任务上表现出相对于其他最先进的三重嵌入方法的一致改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过弱监督信号微调预训练知识图谱嵌入，以改进三元组表示方法。
AI.motivation: 现有知识图谱嵌入方法通过聚合函数构建三元组表示时存在语义信息丢失问题，尤其无法有效捕捉谓词语义。
AI.method: 设计微调方法：自动采样三元组并利用预训练模型估计成对相似度，再通过孪生网络架构进行三元组表示优化。
AI.result: 在两个标准知识图谱上，该方法在三元组分类和聚类任务中均优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 弱监督微调策略能有效提升三元组表示性能，证明了预训练嵌入与监督信号结合的可行性。

authors_text: Pablo Barros,Ozge Nilay Yalcın,Ana Tanevska,Alessandra Sciutti
title: Incorporating Rivalry in Reinforcement Learning for a Competitive Game
title_zh: 将竞争纳入强化学习的竞争性游戏中
comment: Accepted at the Neural Computing and Applications Journal
comments: Accepted at the Neural Computing and Applications Journal
abstract: Recent advances in reinforcement learning with social agents have allowed such models to achieve human-level performance on specific interaction tasks. However, most interactive scenarios do not have a version alone as an end goal; instead, the social impact of these agents when interacting with humans is as important and largely unexplored. In this regard, this work proposes a novel reinforcement learning mechanism based on the social impact of rivalry behavior. Our proposed model aggregates objective and social perception mechanisms to derive a rivalry score that is used to modulate the learning of artificial agents. To investigate our proposed model, we design an interactive game scenario, using the Chef's Hat Card Game, and examine how the rivalry modulation changes the agent's playing style, and how this impacts the experience of human players in the game. Our results show that humans can detect specific social characteristics when playing against rival agents when compared to common agents, which directly affects the performance of the human players in subsequent games. We conclude our work by discussing how the different social and objective features that compose the artificial rivalry score contribute to our results.
summary: Recent advances in reinforcement learning with social agents have allowed such models to achieve human-level performance on specific interaction tasks. However, most interactive scenarios do not have a version alone as an end goal; instead, the social impact of these agents when interacting with humans is as important and largely unexplored. In this regard, this work proposes a novel reinforcement learning mechanism based on the social impact of rivalry behavior. Our proposed model aggregates objective and social perception mechanisms to derive a rivalry score that is used to modulate the learning of artificial agents. To investigate our proposed model, we design an interactive game scenario, using the Chef's Hat Card Game, and examine how the rivalry modulation changes the agent's playing style, and how this impacts the experience of human players in the game. Our results show that humans can detect specific social characteristics when playing against rival agents when compared to common agents, which directly affects the performance of the human players in subsequent games. We conclude our work by discussing how the different social and objective features that compose the artificial rivalry score contribute to our results.
abstract_zh: 社交代理强化学习的最新进展使此类模型能够在特定交互任务上实现人类水平的表现。然而，大多数交互场景并没有单独的版本作为最终目标；相反，这些代理在与人类互动时的社会影响同样重要，而且在很大程度上尚未被探索。在这方面，这项工作提出了一种基于竞争行为的社会影响的新型强化学习机制。我们提出的模型聚合了客观和社会感知机制，以得出用于调节人工智能体学习的竞争分数。为了研究我们提出的模型，我们使用厨师帽纸牌游戏设计了一个交互式游戏场景，并检查竞争调制如何改变代理的游戏风格，以及这如何影响人类玩家在游戏中的体验。我们的结果表明，与普通智能体相比，人类在与对手智能体对战时可以检测到特定的社交特征，这直接影响人类玩家在后续游戏中的表现。我们通过讨论构成人工竞争得分的不同社会和客观特征如何对我们的结果做出贡献来结束我们的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合社会影响的竞争机制，用于强化学习中的竞争性游戏场景，通过设计卡牌游戏验证该机制对人类玩家体验的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有强化学习模型在交互任务中忽视了社交影响的重要性，而人类在竞争场景中的社会特征对互动效果有显著影响，需探索如何将竞争行为纳入学习机制。
AI.method: 构建融合客观目标与社会感知的双重机制，生成竞争分数以调节智能体学习；通过厨师帽卡牌游戏验证竞争调节能否改变智能体策略及影响人类玩家表现。
AI.result: 实验表明人类玩家能区分竞争型智能体与普通智能体的社会特征，且这种区分直接影响其后续游戏表现。
AI.conclusion: 竞争分数由社会与目标特征共同构成，社会特征的引入显著增强了智能体与人类的交互质量，为社交强化学习提供新方向。

authors_text: Gary C. F. Lee,Amir Weiss,Alejandro Lancho,Jennifer Tang,Yuheng Bu,Yury Polyanskiy,Gregory W. Wornell
title: Exploiting Temporal Structures of Cyclostationary Signals for Data-Driven Single-Channel Source Separation
title_zh: 利用循环平稳信号的时间结构进行数据驱动的单通道源分离
abstract: We study the problem of single-channel source separation (SCSS), and focus on cyclostationary signals, which are particularly suitable in a variety of application domains. Unlike classical SCSS approaches, we consider a setting where only examples of the sources are available rather than their models, inspiring a data-driven approach. For source models with underlying cyclostationary Gaussian constituents, we establish a lower bound on the attainable mean squared error (MSE) for any separation method, model-based or data-driven. Our analysis further reveals the operation for optimal separation and the associated implementation challenges. As a computationally attractive alternative, we propose a deep learning approach using a U-Net architecture, which is competitive with the minimum MSE estimator. We demonstrate in simulation that, with suitable domain-informed architectural choices, our U-Net method can approach the optimal performance with substantially reduced computational burden.
summary: We study the problem of single-channel source separation (SCSS), and focus on cyclostationary signals, which are particularly suitable in a variety of application domains. Unlike classical SCSS approaches, we consider a setting where only examples of the sources are available rather than their models, inspiring a data-driven approach. For source models with underlying cyclostationary Gaussian constituents, we establish a lower bound on the attainable mean squared error (MSE) for any separation method, model-based or data-driven. Our analysis further reveals the operation for optimal separation and the associated implementation challenges. As a computationally attractive alternative, we propose a deep learning approach using a U-Net architecture, which is competitive with the minimum MSE estimator. We demonstrate in simulation that, with suitable domain-informed architectural choices, our U-Net method can approach the optimal performance with substantially reduced computational burden.
abstract_zh: 我们研究单通道源分离（SCSS）问题，并重点关注循环平稳信号，它特别适合各种应用领域。与经典的 SCSS 方法不同，我们考虑的设置是仅提供源示例而不是模型，从而激发了数据驱动的方法。对于具有基础循环平稳高斯成分的源模型，我们为任何基于模型或数据驱动的分离方法建立了可达到的均方误差（MSE）的下界。我们的分析进一步揭示了最佳分离的操作以及相关的实施挑战。作为一种计算上有吸引力的替代方案，我们提出了一种使用 U-Net 架构的深度学习方法，该方法与最小 MSE 估计器具有竞争力。我们在模拟中证明，通过适当的领域知情架构选择，我们的 U-Net 方法可以在显着减少计算负担的情况下达到最佳性能。

AI.tldr: 本文研究基于数据驱动的单通道源分离问题，利用循环平稳信号的时间结构特性，提出了一种深度学习方法。
AI.motivation: 传统单通道源分离方法依赖信号模型，而实际应用中常缺乏精确模型。循环平稳信号在多个领域有广泛应用，本文旨在通过数据驱动方法解决该问题，并建立均方误差下界以指导方法设计。
AI.method: 提出基于U-Net架构的深度学习方法，结合领域知识设计网络结构，并与最小均方误差估计器进行对比验证。
AI.result: 仿真结果表明，所提方法在计算负担显著降低的情况下，性能可接近理论最优下界，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文证明了利用循环平稳信号时域结构的深度学习方法在单通道源分离任务中的可行性，为后续研究提供了理论分析与高效实现方案。

search_text: Exploiting Temporal Structures of Cyclostationary Signals for Data-Driven Single-Channel Source Separation 利用循环平稳信号的时间结构进行数据驱动的单通道源分离 We study the problem of single-channel source separation (SCSS), and focus on cyclostationary signals, which are particularly suitable in a variety of application domains. Unlike classical SCSS approaches, we consider a setting where only examples of the sources are available rather than their models, inspiring a data-driven approach. For source models with underlying cyclostationary Gaussian constituents, we establish a lower bound on the attainable mean squared error (MSE) for any separation method, model-based or data-driven. Our analysis further reveals the operation for optimal separation and the associated implementation challenges. As a computationally attractive alternative, we propose a deep learning approach using a U-Net architecture, which is competitive with the minimum MSE estimator. We demonstrate in simulation that, with suitable domain-informed architectural choices, our U-Net method can approach the optimal performance with substantially reduced computational burden. 我们研究单通道源分离（SCSS）问题，并重点关注循环平稳信号，它特别适合各种应用领域。与经典的 SCSS 方法不同，我们考虑的设置是仅提供源示例而不是模型，从而激发了数据驱动的方法。对于具有基础循环平稳高斯成分的源模型，我们为任何基于模型或数据驱动的分离方法建立了可达到的均方误差（MSE）的下界。我们的分析进一步揭示了最佳分离的操作以及相关的实施挑战。作为一种计算上有吸引力的替代方案，我们提出了一种使用 U-Net 架构的深度学习方法，该方法与最小 MSE 估计器具有竞争力。我们在模拟中证明，通过适当的领域知情架构选择，我们的 U-Net 方法可以在显着减少计算负担的情况下达到最佳性能。 本文研究基于数据驱动的单通道源分离问题，利用循环平稳信号的时间结构特性，提出了一种深度学习方法。 Gary C. F. Lee,Amir Weiss,Alejandro Lancho,Jennifer Tang,Yuheng Bu,Yury Polyanskiy,Gregory W. Wornell eess.SP,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Shahab Aslani,Watjana Lilaonitkul,Vaishnavi Gnanananthan,Divya Raj,Bojidar Rangelov,Alexandra L Young,Yipeng Hu,Paul Taylor,Daniel C Alexander,Joseph Jacob
title: Optimising Chest X-Rays for Image Analysis by Identifying and Removing Confounding Factors
title_zh: 通过识别和消除混杂因素优化胸部 X 光图像分析
abstract: During the COVID-19 pandemic, the sheer volume of imaging performed in an emergency setting for COVID-19 diagnosis has resulted in a wide variability of clinical CXR acquisitions. This variation is seen in the CXR projections used, image annotations added and in the inspiratory effort and degree of rotation of clinical images. The image analysis community has attempted to ease the burden on overstretched radiology departments during the pandemic by developing automated COVID-19 diagnostic algorithms, the input for which has been CXR imaging. Large publicly available CXR datasets have been leveraged to improve deep learning algorithms for COVID-19 diagnosis. Yet the variable quality of clinically-acquired CXRs within publicly available datasets could have a profound effect on algorithm performance. COVID-19 diagnosis may be inferred by an algorithm from non-anatomical features on an image such as image labels. These imaging shortcuts may be dataset-specific and limit the generalisability of AI systems. Understanding and correcting key potential biases in CXR images is therefore an essential first step prior to CXR image analysis. In this study, we propose a simple and effective step-wise approach to pre-processing a COVID-19 chest X-ray dataset to remove undesired biases. We perform ablation studies to show the impact of each individual step. The results suggest that using our proposed pipeline could increase accuracy of the baseline COVID-19 detection algorithm by up to 13%.
summary: During the COVID-19 pandemic, the sheer volume of imaging performed in an emergency setting for COVID-19 diagnosis has resulted in a wide variability of clinical CXR acquisitions. This variation is seen in the CXR projections used, image annotations added and in the inspiratory effort and degree of rotation of clinical images. The image analysis community has attempted to ease the burden on overstretched radiology departments during the pandemic by developing automated COVID-19 diagnostic algorithms, the input for which has been CXR imaging. Large publicly available CXR datasets have been leveraged to improve deep learning algorithms for COVID-19 diagnosis. Yet the variable quality of clinically-acquired CXRs within publicly available datasets could have a profound effect on algorithm performance. COVID-19 diagnosis may be inferred by an algorithm from non-anatomical features on an image such as image labels. These imaging shortcuts may be dataset-specific and limit the generalisability of AI systems. Understanding and correcting key potential biases in CXR images is therefore an essential first step prior to CXR image analysis. In this study, we propose a simple and effective step-wise approach to pre-processing a COVID-19 chest X-ray dataset to remove undesired biases. We perform ablation studies to show the impact of each individual step. The results suggest that using our proposed pipeline could increase accuracy of the baseline COVID-19 detection algorithm by up to 13%.
abstract_zh: 在 COVID-19 大流行期间，在紧急情况下进行的用于 COVID-19 诊断的大量成像导致临床 CXR 采集存在很大差异。这种变化体现在使用的 CXR 投影、添加的图像注释以及临床图像的吸气力度和旋转程度中。图像分析界试图通过开发自动化的 COVID-19 诊断算法（其输入是 CXR 成像）来减轻大流行期间过度紧张的放射科的负担。大型公开可用的 CXR 数据集已被用来改进用于 COVID-19 诊断的深度学习算法。然而，公开数据集中临床获取的 CXR 的质量参差不齐，可能会对算法性能产生深远影响。 COVID-19 诊断可以通过算法根据图像上的非解剖特征（例如图像标签）来推断。这些成像快捷方式可能是特定于数据集的，并限制了人工智能系统的通用性。因此，了解并纠正 CXR 图像中的关键潜在偏差是 CXR 图像分析之前必不可少的第一步。在这项研究中，我们提出了一种简单有效的逐步方法来预处理 COVID-19 胸部 X 射线数据集，以消除不需要的偏差。我们进行消融研究以显示每个步骤的影响。结果表明，使用我们提出的管道可以将基线 COVID-19 检测算法的准确性提高高达 13%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分步预处理方法，通过消除胸部X光图像中的混淆因素以优化图像分析。
AI.motivation: 疫情期间临床获取的胸部X光图像存在投影方式、标注信息及呼吸状态等变异，导致公开数据集质量参差不齐，影响AI诊断算法的泛化能力。
AI.method: 设计包含图像标注修正、呼吸状态筛选及旋转校正的预处理流程，并通过消融实验验证各步骤效果。
AI.result: 所提预处理流程使基线新冠检测算法准确率提升最高达13%。
AI.conclusion: 消除胸部X光图像中的关键偏差因素可显著提升AI诊断性能，为临床影像分析提供可靠数据基础。

authors_text: Cyprien Gille,Frederic Guyard,Michel Barlaud
title: Semi-supervised classification using a supervised autoencoder for biomedical applications
title_zh: 使用监督自动编码器进行生物医学应用的半监督分类
abstract: In this paper we present a new approach to solve semi-supervised classification tasks for biomedical applications, involving a supervised autoencoder network. We create a network architecture that encodes labels into the latent space of an autoencoder, and define a global criterion combining classification and reconstruction losses. We train the Semi-Supervised AutoEncoder (SSAE) on labelled data using a double descent algorithm. Then, we classify unlabelled samples using the learned network thanks to a softmax classifier applied to the latent space which provides a classification confidence score for each class. We implemented our SSAE method using the PyTorch framework for the model, optimizer, schedulers, and loss functions. We compare our semi-supervised autoencoder method (SSAE) with classical semi-supervised methods such as Label Propagation and Label Spreading, and with a Fully Connected Neural Network (FCNN). Experiments show that the SSAE outperforms Label Propagation and Spreading and the Fully Connected Neural Network both on a synthetic dataset and on two real-world biological datasets.
summary: In this paper we present a new approach to solve semi-supervised classification tasks for biomedical applications, involving a supervised autoencoder network. We create a network architecture that encodes labels into the latent space of an autoencoder, and define a global criterion combining classification and reconstruction losses. We train the Semi-Supervised AutoEncoder (SSAE) on labelled data using a double descent algorithm. Then, we classify unlabelled samples using the learned network thanks to a softmax classifier applied to the latent space which provides a classification confidence score for each class. We implemented our SSAE method using the PyTorch framework for the model, optimizer, schedulers, and loss functions. We compare our semi-supervised autoencoder method (SSAE) with classical semi-supervised methods such as Label Propagation and Label Spreading, and with a Fully Connected Neural Network (FCNN). Experiments show that the SSAE outperforms Label Propagation and Spreading and the Fully Connected Neural Network both on a synthetic dataset and on two real-world biological datasets.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种解决生物医学应用的半监督分类任务的新方法，涉及监督自动编码器网络。我们创建了一个网络架构，将标签编码到自动编码器的潜在空间中，并定义了一个结合分类和重建损失的全局标准。我们使用双下降算法在标记数据上训练半监督自动编码器（SSAE）。然后，我们使用学习网络对未标记的样本进行分类，这要归功于应用于潜在空间的 softmax 分类器，该分类器为每个类别提供了分类置信度得分。我们使用 PyTorch 框架来实现 SSAE 方法的模型、优化器、调度器和损失函数。我们将半监督自动编码器方法（SSAE）与经典的半监督方法（例如标签传播和标签传播）以及全连接神经网络（FCNN）进行比较。实验表明，SSAE 在合成数据集和两个真实生物数据集上均优于标签传播和传播以及全连接神经网络。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于监督自编码器的半监督分类方法（SSAE），用于解决生物医学领域的分类问题。
AI.motivation: 生物医学数据通常存在标签稀缺问题，传统半监督方法在复杂数据上表现有限，需更有效的模型利用有限标签和未标记数据。
AI.method: 设计将标签编码至自编码器潜在空间的网络架构，结合分类损失与重构损失定义全局目标函数，采用双重下降算法训练模型，并通过PyTorch框架实现。
AI.result: 在合成数据集和两个真实生物数据集上，SSAE分类准确率显著优于标签传播、标签扩散及全连接神经网络。
AI.conclusion: SSAE通过联合优化分类与重构任务，有效提升半监督学习性能，适用于生物医学等标签稀缺领域的分类任务。

motivation: 生物医学数据通常存在标签稀缺问题，传统半监督方法在复杂数据上表现有限，需更有效的模型利用有限标签和未标记数据。
method: 设计将标签编码至自编码器潜在空间的网络架构，结合分类损失与重构损失定义全局目标函数，采用双重下降算法训练模型，并通过PyTorch框架实现。
result: 在合成数据集和两个真实生物数据集上，SSAE分类准确率显著优于标签传播、标签扩散及全连接神经网络。
conclusion: SSAE通过联合优化分类与重构任务，有效提升半监督学习性能，适用于生物医学等标签稀缺领域的分类任务。
search_text: Semi-supervised classification using a supervised autoencoder for biomedical applications 使用监督自动编码器进行生物医学应用的半监督分类 In this paper we present a new approach to solve semi-supervised classification tasks for biomedical applications, involving a supervised autoencoder network. We create a network architecture that encodes labels into the latent space of an autoencoder, and define a global criterion combining classification and reconstruction losses. We train the Semi-Supervised AutoEncoder (SSAE) on labelled data using a double descent algorithm. Then, we classify unlabelled samples using the learned network thanks to a softmax classifier applied to the latent space which provides a classification confidence score for each class. We implemented our SSAE method using the PyTorch framework for the model, optimizer, schedulers, and loss functions. We compare our semi-supervised autoencoder method (SSAE) with classical semi-supervised methods such as Label Propagation and Label Spreading, and with a Fully Connected Neural Network (FCNN). Experiments show that the SSAE outperforms Label Propagation and Spreading and the Fully Connected Neural Network both on a synthetic dataset and on two real-world biological datasets. 在本文中，我们提出了一种解决生物医学应用的半监督分类任务的新方法，涉及监督自动编码器网络。我们创建了一个网络架构，将标签编码到自动编码器的潜在空间中，并定义了一个结合分类和重建损失的全局标准。我们使用双下降算法在标记数据上训练半监督自动编码器（SSAE）。然后，我们使用学习网络对未标记的样本进行分类，这要归功于应用于潜在空间的 softmax 分类器，该分类器为每个类别提供了分类置信度得分。我们使用 PyTorch 框架来实现 SSAE 方法的模型、优化器、调度器和损失函数。我们将半监督自动编码器方法（SSAE）与经典的半监督方法（例如标签传播和标签传播）以及全连接神经网络（FCNN）进行比较。实验表明，SSAE 在合成数据集和两个真实生物数据集上均优于标签传播和传播以及全连接神经网络。 本文提出一种基于监督自编码器的半监督分类方法（SSAE），用于解决生物医学领域的分类问题。 Cyprien Gille,Frederic Guyard,Michel Barlaud cs.LG,cs.AI,q-bio.GN

authors_text: Yury Kaminsky,Irina Deeva
title: BigBraveBN: algorithm of structural learning for bayesian networks with a large number of nodes
title_zh: BigBraveBN：具有大量节点的贝叶斯网络的结构学习算法
comment: The article contains 10 pages and 10 figures
comments: The article contains 10 pages and 10 figures
abstract: Learning a Bayesian network is an NP-hard problem and with an increase in the number of nodes, classical algorithms for learning the structure of Bayesian networks become inefficient. In recent years, some methods and algorithms for learning Bayesian networks with a high number of nodes (more than 50) were developed. But these solutions have their disadvantages, for instance, they only operate one type of data (discrete or continuous) or their algorithm has been created to meet a specific nature of data (medical, social, etc.). The article presents a BigBraveBN algorithm for learning large Bayesian Networks with a high number of nodes (over 100). The algorithm utilizes the Brave coefficient that measures the mutual occurrence of instances in several groups. To form these groups, we use the method of nearest neighbours based on the Mutual information (MI) measure. In the experimental part of the article, we compare the performance of BigBraveBN to other existing solutions on multiple data sets both discrete and continuous. The experimental part also represents tests on real data. The aforementioned experimental results demonstrate the efficiency of the BigBraveBN algorithm in structure learning of Bayesian Networks.
summary: Learning a Bayesian network is an NP-hard problem and with an increase in the number of nodes, classical algorithms for learning the structure of Bayesian networks become inefficient. In recent years, some methods and algorithms for learning Bayesian networks with a high number of nodes (more than 50) were developed. But these solutions have their disadvantages, for instance, they only operate one type of data (discrete or continuous) or their algorithm has been created to meet a specific nature of data (medical, social, etc.). The article presents a BigBraveBN algorithm for learning large Bayesian Networks with a high number of nodes (over 100). The algorithm utilizes the Brave coefficient that measures the mutual occurrence of instances in several groups. To form these groups, we use the method of nearest neighbours based on the Mutual information (MI) measure. In the experimental part of the article, we compare the performance of BigBraveBN to other existing solutions on multiple data sets both discrete and continuous. The experimental part also represents tests on real data. The aforementioned experimental results demonstrate the efficiency of the BigBraveBN algorithm in structure learning of Bayesian Networks.
abstract_zh: 学习贝叶斯网络是一个NP难题，随着节点数量的增加，学习贝叶斯网络结构的经典算法变得低效。近年来，一些用于学习具有大量节点（超过50个）的贝叶斯网络的方法和算法被开发出来。但这些解决方案也有其缺点，例如，它们仅操作一种类型的数据（离散或连续），或者它们的算法是为了满足数据的特定性质（医疗、社交等）而创建的。本文提出了一种 BigBraveBN 算法，用于学习具有大量节点（超过 100 个）的大型贝叶斯网络。该算法利用 Brave 系数来衡量多个组中实例的相互出现情况。为了形成这些组，我们使用基于互信息 (MI) 度量的最近邻方法。在本文的实验部分，我们将 BigBraveBN 与其他现有解决方案在多个离散和连续数据集上的性能进行了比较。实验部分也代表对真实数据的测试。上述实验结果证明了BigBraveBN算法在贝叶斯网络结构学习中的有效性。

AI.tldr: 提出BigBraveBN算法，用于高效学习具有超过100个节点的大规模贝叶斯网络结构。
AI.motivation: 现有算法在处理高节点数（>50）贝叶斯网络时存在局限性，如仅支持单一数据类型（离散或连续）或特定领域数据，难以满足大规模网络结构学习需求。
AI.method: 基于Brave系数衡量实例在不同组间的共现关系，结合互信息（MI）的最近邻方法进行分组，从而优化大规模贝叶斯网络结构学习。
AI.result: 实验表明，BigBraveBN在多种离散、连续数据集及真实数据上均优于现有算法，验证了其高效性与通用性。
AI.conclusion: BigBraveBN算法通过创新分组策略有效解决了大规模贝叶斯网络结构学习的效率问题，适用于多类型数据场景。

authors_text: Fengying Dang,Dong Chen,Jun Chen,Zhaojian Li
title: Event-Triggered Model Predictive Control with Deep Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
title_zh: 用于自动驾驶的事件触发模型预测控制和深度强化学习
abstract: Event-triggered model predictive control (eMPC) is a popular optimal control method with an aim to alleviate the computation and/or communication burden of MPC. However, it generally requires priori knowledge of the closed-loop system behavior along with the communication characteristics for designing the event-trigger policy. This paper attempts to solve this challenge by proposing an efficient eMPC framework and demonstrate successful implementation of this framework on the autonomous vehicle path following. First of all, a model-free reinforcement learning (RL) agent is used to learn the optimal event-trigger policy without the need for a complete dynamical system and communication knowledge in this framework. Furthermore, techniques including prioritized experience replay (PER) buffer and long-short term memory (LSTM) are employed to foster exploration and improve training efficiency. In this paper, we use the proposed framework with three deep RL algorithms, i.e., Double Q-learning (DDQN), Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), to solve this problem. Experimental results show that all three deep RL-based eMPC (deep-RL-eMPC) can achieve better evaluation performance than the conventional threshold-based and previous linear Q-based approach in the autonomous path following. In particular, PPO-eMPC with LSTM and DDQN-eMPC with PER and LSTM obtains a superior balance between the closed-loop control performance and event-trigger frequency. The associated code is open-sourced and available at: https://github.com/DangFengying/RL-based-event-triggered-MPC.
summary: Event-triggered model predictive control (eMPC) is a popular optimal control method with an aim to alleviate the computation and/or communication burden of MPC. However, it generally requires priori knowledge of the closed-loop system behavior along with the communication characteristics for designing the event-trigger policy. This paper attempts to solve this challenge by proposing an efficient eMPC framework and demonstrate successful implementation of this framework on the autonomous vehicle path following. First of all, a model-free reinforcement learning (RL) agent is used to learn the optimal event-trigger policy without the need for a complete dynamical system and communication knowledge in this framework. Furthermore, techniques including prioritized experience replay (PER) buffer and long-short term memory (LSTM) are employed to foster exploration and improve training efficiency. In this paper, we use the proposed framework with three deep RL algorithms, i.e., Double Q-learning (DDQN), Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), to solve this problem. Experimental results show that all three deep RL-based eMPC (deep-RL-eMPC) can achieve better evaluation performance than the conventional threshold-based and previous linear Q-based approach in the autonomous path following. In particular, PPO-eMPC with LSTM and DDQN-eMPC with PER and LSTM obtains a superior balance between the closed-loop control performance and event-trigger frequency. The associated code is open-sourced and available at: https://github.com/DangFengying/RL-based-event-triggered-MPC.
abstract_zh: 事件触发模型预测控制（eMPC）是一种流行的最优控制方法，旨在减轻 MPC 的计算和/或通信负担。然而，它通常需要先验知识闭环系统行为以及设计事件触发策略的通信特性。本文试图通过提出一种高效的 eMPC 框架来解决这一挑战，并展示该框架在自动驾驶车辆路径跟踪上的成功实施。首先，使用无模型强化学习（RL）代理来学习最佳事件触发策略，而无需在此框架中提供完整的动态系统和通信知识。此外，还采用优先经验重放（PER）缓冲区和长短期记忆（LSTM）等技术来促进探索并提高训练效率。在本文中，我们使用所提出的框架和三种深度强化学习算法，即双 Q 学习（DDQN）、近端策略优化（PPO）和 Soft Actor-Critic（SAC）来解决这个问题。实验结果表明，所有三种基于深度强化学习的 eMPC（deep-RL-eMPC）在自主路径跟踪中都能比传统的基于阈值和之前的基于线性 Q 的方法取得更好的评估性能。特别是，带有 LSTM 的 PPO-eMPC 和带有 PER 和 LSTM 的 DDQN-eMPC 在闭环控制性能和事件触发频率之间获得了良好的平衡。相关代码是开源的，可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/DangFengying/RL-based-event-triggered-MPC。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的事件触发模型预测控制框架，用于自动驾驶车辆的路径跟踪。
AI.motivation: 传统事件触发模型预测控制（eMPC）需要先验的系统行为和通信特性知识，设计复杂且限制较多，本文旨在通过深度强化学习方法解决这一问题。
AI.method: 采用无模型强化学习代理学习事件触发策略，结合优先经验回放（PER）和长短期记忆网络（LSTM）技术，分别使用DDQN、PPO和SAC三种深度强化学习算法实现框架。
AI.result: 实验表明，三种深度RL-eMPC方法均优于传统阈值法和线性Q方法，其中PPO-eMPC（带LSTM）和DDQN-eMPC（带PER+LSTM）在控制性能与事件触发频率间取得最佳平衡。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架有效提升了eMPC的自主决策能力，LSTM与PPO/DDQN的结合在实际应用中表现优异，代码已开源供验证。

search_text: Event-Triggered Model Predictive Control with Deep Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving 用于自动驾驶的事件触发模型预测控制和深度强化学习 Event-triggered model predictive control (eMPC) is a popular optimal control method with an aim to alleviate the computation and/or communication burden of MPC. However, it generally requires priori knowledge of the closed-loop system behavior along with the communication characteristics for designing the event-trigger policy. This paper attempts to solve this challenge by proposing an efficient eMPC framework and demonstrate successful implementation of this framework on the autonomous vehicle path following. First of all, a model-free reinforcement learning (RL) agent is used to learn the optimal event-trigger policy without the need for a complete dynamical system and communication knowledge in this framework. Furthermore, techniques including prioritized experience replay (PER) buffer and long-short term memory (LSTM) are employed to foster exploration and improve training efficiency. In this paper, we use the proposed framework with three deep RL algorithms, i.e., Double Q-learning (DDQN), Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), to solve this problem. Experimental results show that all three deep RL-based eMPC (deep-RL-eMPC) can achieve better evaluation performance than the conventional threshold-based and previous linear Q-based approach in the autonomous path following. In particular, PPO-eMPC with LSTM and DDQN-eMPC with PER and LSTM obtains a superior balance between the closed-loop control performance and event-trigger frequency. The associated code is open-sourced and available at: https://github.com/DangFengying/RL-based-event-triggered-MPC. 事件触发模型预测控制（eMPC）是一种流行的最优控制方法，旨在减轻 MPC 的计算和/或通信负担。然而，它通常需要先验知识闭环系统行为以及设计事件触发策略的通信特性。本文试图通过提出一种高效的 eMPC 框架来解决这一挑战，并展示该框架在自动驾驶车辆路径跟踪上的成功实施。首先，使用无模型强化学习（RL）代理来学习最佳事件触发策略，而无需在此框架中提供完整的动态系统和通信知识。此外，还采用优先经验重放（PER）缓冲区和长短期记忆（LSTM）等技术来促进探索并提高训练效率。在本文中，我们使用所提出的框架和三种深度强化学习算法，即双 Q 学习（DDQN）、近端策略优化（PPO）和 Soft Actor-Critic（SAC）来解决这个问题。实验结果表明，所有三种基于深度强化学习的 eMPC（deep-RL-eMPC）在自主路径跟踪中都能比传统的基于阈值和之前的基于线性 Q 的方法取得更好的评估性能。特别是，带有 LSTM 的 PPO-eMPC 和带有 PER 和 LSTM 的 DDQN-eMPC 在闭环控制性能和事件触发频率之间获得了良好的平衡。相关代码是开源的，可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/DangFengying/RL-based-event-triggered-MPC。 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的事件触发模型预测控制框架，用于自动驾驶车辆的路径跟踪。 Fengying Dang,Dong Chen,Jun Chen,Zhaojian Li cs.RO,eess.SY

authors_text: Farha A. Khan,Tanmay Chakraborty,Jörg P. Dietrich,Christian Wirth
title: Efficient Contextual Preferential Bayesian Optimization with Historical Examples
title_zh: 具有历史示例的高效上下文优先贝叶斯优化
abstract: State-of-the-art multi-objective optimization often assumes a known utility function, learns it interactively, or computes the full Pareto front-each requiring costly expert input.~Real-world problems, however, involve implicit preferences that are hard to formalize. To reduce expert involvement, we propose an offline, interpretable utility learning method that uses expert knowledge, historical examples, and coarse information about the utility space to reduce sample requirements. We model uncertainty via a full Bayesian posterior and propagate it throughout the optimization process. Our method outperforms standard Gaussian processes and BOPE across four domains, showing strong performance even with biased samples, as encountered in the real-world, and limited expert input.
summary: State-of-the-art multi-objective optimization often assumes a known utility function, learns it interactively, or computes the full Pareto front-each requiring costly expert input.~Real-world problems, however, involve implicit preferences that are hard to formalize. To reduce expert involvement, we propose an offline, interpretable utility learning method that uses expert knowledge, historical examples, and coarse information about the utility space to reduce sample requirements. We model uncertainty via a full Bayesian posterior and propagate it throughout the optimization process. Our method outperforms standard Gaussian processes and BOPE across four domains, showing strong performance even with biased samples, as encountered in the real-world, and limited expert input.
abstract_zh: 最先进的多目标优化通常假设一个已知的效用函数，交互式地学习它，或者计算完整的帕累托前沿——每个都需要昂贵的专家输入。然而，现实世界的问题涉及难以形式化的隐式偏好。为了减少专家的参与，我们提出了一种离线的、可解释的效用学习方法，该方法使用专家知识、历史示例和有关效用空间的粗略信息来减少样本需求。我们通过完整的贝叶斯后验对不确定性进行建模，并将其传播到整个优化过程。我们的方法在四个领域都优于标准高斯过程和 BOPE，即使在现实世界中遇到的有偏差的样本和有限的专家输入的情况下，也显示出强大的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合历史示例与贝叶斯优化的方法，通过减少专家参与来解决多目标优化中的隐式偏好问题。
AI.motivation: 现有优化方法依赖专家输入或计算完整帕累托前沿，成本高昂，而实际问题中的隐式偏好难以明确表达，需降低专家依赖并利用历史数据。
AI.method: 采用贝叶斯后验建模不确定性，整合专家知识、历史示例及效用空间粗略信息，在优化过程中传播不确定性以提升效率。
AI.result: 在四个领域中优于标准高斯过程和BOPE方法，即使面对偏差样本和有限专家输入仍保持良好性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效降低样本需求，适用于现实场景中专家资源有限且偏好隐含的复杂优化问题。

authors_text: Vincent Wall,Gabriel Zöller,Oliver Brock
title: Passive and Active Acoustic Sensing for Soft Pneumatic Actuators
title_zh: 软气动执行器的被动和主动声学传感
comment: This paper is currently under review in The International Journal of Robotics Research
comments: This paper is currently under review in The International Journal of Robotics Research
abstract: We propose a sensorization method for soft pneumatic actuators that uses an embedded microphone and speaker to measure different actuator properties. The physical state of the actuator determines the specific modulation of sound as it travels through the structure. Using simple machine learning, we create a computational sensor that infers the corresponding state from sound recordings. We demonstrate the acoustic sensor on a soft pneumatic continuum actuator and use it to measure contact locations, contact forces, object materials, actuator inflation, and actuator temperature. We show that the sensor is reliable (average classification rate for six contact locations of 93%), precise (mean spatial accuracy of 3.7 mm), and robust against common disturbances like background noise. Finally, we compare different sounds and learning methods and achieve best results with 20 ms of white noise and a support vector classifier as the sensor model.
summary: We propose a sensorization method for soft pneumatic actuators that uses an embedded microphone and speaker to measure different actuator properties. The physical state of the actuator determines the specific modulation of sound as it travels through the structure. Using simple machine learning, we create a computational sensor that infers the corresponding state from sound recordings. We demonstrate the acoustic sensor on a soft pneumatic continuum actuator and use it to measure contact locations, contact forces, object materials, actuator inflation, and actuator temperature. We show that the sensor is reliable (average classification rate for six contact locations of 93%), precise (mean spatial accuracy of 3.7 mm), and robust against common disturbances like background noise. Finally, we compare different sounds and learning methods and achieve best results with 20 ms of white noise and a support vector classifier as the sensor model.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用于软气动执行器的传感方法，该方法使用嵌入式麦克风和扬声器来测量不同的执行器特性。执行器的物理状态决定了声音穿过结构时的具体调制。使用简单的机器学习，我们创建了一个计算传感器，可以从录音中推断出相应的状态。我们在软气动连续体执行器上演示了声学传感器，并用它来测量接触位置、接触力、物体材料、执行器充气和执行器温度。我们证明该传感器可靠（六个接触位置的平均分类率为 93%）、精确（平均空间精度为 3.7 毫米），并且对背景噪声等常见干扰具有鲁棒性。最后，我们比较了不同的声音和学习方法，并以 20 ms 的白噪声和支持向量分类器作为传感器模型获得了最佳结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用嵌入式麦克风和扬声器的声学传感方法，通过机器学习实现对软气动执行器状态的非接触式监测。
AI.motivation: 传统传感器存在成本高或需物理接触的局限，而声学传感可提供非接触、低成本的替代方案，同时具备高可靠性和抗干扰能力。
AI.method: 通过嵌入麦克风和扬声器采集声学信号，利用机器学习模型（如支持向量分类器）分析声音调制特征，结合20ms白噪声输入实现状态推断。
AI.result: 实现93%的接触位置分类准确率、3.7mm空间精度，且对背景噪声具有鲁棒性，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该声学传感方法可可靠测量执行器多参数，为软体机器人提供了新型感知方案，具有实际应用潜力。

authors_text: Tianpeng Gao,Jianzhong Li
title: Approximate sorting and its application in I/O model
title_zh: 近似排序及其在I/O模型中的应用
abstract: The approximate sorting for big data is considered in this paper. The goal of approximate sorting for big data is to generate an approximate sorted result, but using less CPU and I/O cost. For big data, we consider the approximate sorting in I/O model. The existing metrics on permutation space are not available for external approximate sorting algorithms. Thus, we propose a new kind of metric named External metric, which ignores the errors and dislocation that happened in each I/O block.The External Spearmans footrule metric is an example of external metric for Spearmans footrule metric. Furthermore, to facilitate a better evaluation of the approximate sorted result, we propose a new metric, named as errors, which directly states the number of dislocation of the elements. Its external metric external errors is also considered in this paper. Then, according to the rate-distortion relationship endowed by these two metrics, the lower bound of these two metrics on external approximate sorting problem with t I/O operations is proved. We propose a k-pass external approximate sorting algorithm, named as EASORT, and prove that EASORT is asymptotically optimal. Finally, we consider the applications on approximate sorting results. An index for the result of our approximate sorting is proposed and analyze the single and range query on approximate sorted result using this index. Further, the sort-merge join on two relations, where one of the relations is approximate sorted or both relations are approximate sorted, are all discussed in this paper.
summary: The approximate sorting for big data is considered in this paper. The goal of approximate sorting for big data is to generate an approximate sorted result, but using less CPU and I/O cost. For big data, we consider the approximate sorting in I/O model. The existing metrics on permutation space are not available for external approximate sorting algorithms. Thus, we propose a new kind of metric named External metric, which ignores the errors and dislocation that happened in each I/O block.The External Spearmans footrule metric is an example of external metric for Spearmans footrule metric. Furthermore, to facilitate a better evaluation of the approximate sorted result, we propose a new metric, named as errors, which directly states the number of dislocation of the elements. Its external metric external errors is also considered in this paper. Then, according to the rate-distortion relationship endowed by these two metrics, the lower bound of these two metrics on external approximate sorting problem with t I/O operations is proved. We propose a k-pass external approximate sorting algorithm, named as EASORT, and prove that EASORT is asymptotically optimal. Finally, we consider the applications on approximate sorting results. An index for the result of our approximate sorting is proposed and analyze the single and range query on approximate sorted result using this index. Further, the sort-merge join on two relations, where one of the relations is approximate sorted or both relations are approximate sorted, are all discussed in this paper.
abstract_zh: 本文考虑的是大数据的近似排序。大数据近似排序的目标是生成近似排序结果，但使用较少的 CPU 和 I/O 成本。对于大数据，我们考虑I/O模型中的近似排序。排列空间的现有度量不可用于外部近似排序算法。因此，我们提出了一种新的度量，称为外部度量，它忽略了每个 I/O 块中发生的错误和错位。外部 Spearmans footrule 度量是 Spearmans footrule 度量的外部度量的一个示例。此外，为了更好地评估近似排序结果，我们提出了一个新的度量，称为错误，它直接说明元素错位的数量。本文还考虑了其​​外部度量的外部误差。然后，根据这两个度量所赋予的速率-失真关系，证明了这两个度量在具有t I/O操作的外部近似排序问题上的下界。我们提出了一种k遍外部近似排序算法，命名为EASORT，并证明EASORT是渐近最优的。最后，我们考虑在近似排序结果上的应用。提出了近似排序结果的索引，并利用该索引分析了近似排序结果的单次查询和范围查询。此外，本文还讨论了两个关系上的排序合并连接，其中一个关系是近似排序的，或者两个关系都是近似排序的。

AI.tldr: 本文研究大数据的近似排序方法，旨在减少CPU和I/O成本，并提出新的评估指标与算法。
AI.motivation: 传统排序指标无法有效评估外部近似排序的误差，需设计适用于I/O模型的新度量标准以优化大数据处理效率。
AI.method: 提出外部度量（External metric）和错误数指标，定义外部Spearman脚规度量与外部错误度量，并设计k-pass近似排序算法EASORT。
AI.result: 证明两种指标在t次I/O操作下的下界，EASORT算法被证明在渐进意义上最优，并分析了其在索引构建与连接操作中的应用。
AI.conclusion: 新指标与算法有效平衡了排序精度与I/O成本，为大数据近似处理提供了理论支持与实用工具。

search_text: Approximate sorting and its application in I/O model 近似排序及其在I/O模型中的应用 The approximate sorting for big data is considered in this paper. The goal of approximate sorting for big data is to generate an approximate sorted result, but using less CPU and I/O cost. For big data, we consider the approximate sorting in I/O model. The existing metrics on permutation space are not available for external approximate sorting algorithms. Thus, we propose a new kind of metric named External metric, which ignores the errors and dislocation that happened in each I/O block.The External Spearmans footrule metric is an example of external metric for Spearmans footrule metric. Furthermore, to facilitate a better evaluation of the approximate sorted result, we propose a new metric, named as errors, which directly states the number of dislocation of the elements. Its external metric external errors is also considered in this paper. Then, according to the rate-distortion relationship endowed by these two metrics, the lower bound of these two metrics on external approximate sorting problem with t I/O operations is proved. We propose a k-pass external approximate sorting algorithm, named as EASORT, and prove that EASORT is asymptotically optimal. Finally, we consider the applications on approximate sorting results. An index for the result of our approximate sorting is proposed and analyze the single and range query on approximate sorted result using this index. Further, the sort-merge join on two relations, where one of the relations is approximate sorted or both relations are approximate sorted, are all discussed in this paper. 本文考虑的是大数据的近似排序。大数据近似排序的目标是生成近似排序结果，但使用较少的 CPU 和 I/O 成本。对于大数据，我们考虑I/O模型中的近似排序。排列空间的现有度量不可用于外部近似排序算法。因此，我们提出了一种新的度量，称为外部度量，它忽略了每个 I/O 块中发生的错误和错位。外部 Spearmans footrule 度量是 Spearmans footrule 度量的外部度量的一个示例。此外，为了更好地评估近似排序结果，我们提出了一个新的度量，称为错误，它直接说明元素错位的数量。本文还考虑了其​​外部度量的外部误差。然后，根据这两个度量所赋予的速率-失真关系，证明了这两个度量在具有t I/O操作的外部近似排序问题上的下界。我们提出了一种k遍外部近似排序算法，命名为EASORT，并证明EASORT是渐近最优的。最后，我们考虑在近似排序结果上的应用。提出了近似排序结果的索引，并利用该索引分析了近似排序结果的单次查询和范围查询。此外，本文还讨论了两个关系上的排序合并连接，其中一个关系是近似排序的，或者两个关系都是近似排序的。 本文研究大数据的近似排序方法，旨在减少CPU和I/O成本，并提出新的评估指标与算法。 Tianpeng Gao,Jianzhong Li cs.DS,cs.DB,cs.IT

authors_text: Wouter Jansen,Nico Huebel,Jan Steckel
title: Physical LiDAR Simulation in Real-Time Engine
title_zh: 实时引擎中的物理 LiDAR 仿真
comment: IEEE Sensors 2022 Conference, Dallas, TX, USA
comments: IEEE Sensors 2022 Conference, Dallas, TX, USA
abstract: Designing and validating sensor applications and algorithms in simulation is an important step in the modern development process. Furthermore, modern open-source multi-sensor simulation frameworks are moving towards the usage of video-game engines such as the Unreal Engine. Simulation of a sensor such as a LiDAR can prove to be difficult in such real-time software. In this paper we present a GPU-accelerated simulation of LiDAR based on its physical properties and interaction with the environment. We provide a generation of the depth and intensity data based on the properties of the sensor as well as the surface material and incidence angle at which the light beams hit the surface. It is validated against a real LiDAR sensor and shown to be accurate and precise although highly depended on the spectral data used for the material properties.
summary: Designing and validating sensor applications and algorithms in simulation is an important step in the modern development process. Furthermore, modern open-source multi-sensor simulation frameworks are moving towards the usage of video-game engines such as the Unreal Engine. Simulation of a sensor such as a LiDAR can prove to be difficult in such real-time software. In this paper we present a GPU-accelerated simulation of LiDAR based on its physical properties and interaction with the environment. We provide a generation of the depth and intensity data based on the properties of the sensor as well as the surface material and incidence angle at which the light beams hit the surface. It is validated against a real LiDAR sensor and shown to be accurate and precise although highly depended on the spectral data used for the material properties.
abstract_zh: 在仿真中设计和验证传感器应用和算法是现代开发过程中的重要一步。此外，现代开源多传感器模拟框架正在转向使用虚幻引擎等视频游戏引擎。在此类实时软件中模拟激光雷达等传感器可能很困难。在本文中，我们基于 LiDAR 的物理特性和与环境的交互，提出了一种 GPU 加速的 LiDAR 仿真。我们根据传感器的特性以及表面材料和光束撞击表面的入射角提供深度和强度数据的生成。它针对真实的 LiDAR 传感器进行了验证，尽管高度依赖于用于材料特性的光谱数据，但显示出准确和精确。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物理特性的实时引擎中LiDAR传感器的GPU加速模拟方法。
AI.motivation: 现代开发流程中需要通过模拟验证传感器算法，但实时引擎中的LiDAR模拟因复杂性而面临挑战。
AI.method: 利用GPU加速，根据传感器特性、表面材质及入射角生成深度和强度数据，并通过真实LiDAR验证。
AI.result: 模拟结果在精度和准确性上表现良好，但高度依赖材料的光谱数据质量。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为实时引擎中的LiDAR模拟提供了有效方案，但需注意光谱数据的准确性。

authors_text: Peter Belcak,Roger Wattenhofer
title: Deterministic Graph-Walking Program Mining
title_zh: 确定性图行走程序挖掘
comment: Paper accepted for an oral presentation at Advanced Data Mining and Applications (ADMA) 2022. 15 pages, 3 figures
comments: Paper accepted for an oral presentation at Advanced Data Mining and Applications (ADMA) 2022. 15 pages, 3 figures
abstract: Owing to their versatility, graph structures admit representations of intricate relationships between the separate entities comprising the data. We formalise the notion of connection between two vertex sets in terms of edge and vertex features by introducing graph-walking programs. We give two algorithms for mining of deterministic graph-walking programs that yield programs in the order of increasing length. These programs characterise linear long-distance relationships between the given two vertex sets in the context of the whole graph.
summary: Owing to their versatility, graph structures admit representations of intricate relationships between the separate entities comprising the data. We formalise the notion of connection between two vertex sets in terms of edge and vertex features by introducing graph-walking programs. We give two algorithms for mining of deterministic graph-walking programs that yield programs in the order of increasing length. These programs characterise linear long-distance relationships between the given two vertex sets in the context of the whole graph.
abstract_zh: 由于其多功能性，图结构允许表示组成数据的单独实体之间的复杂关系。我们通过引入图行走程序，将两个顶点集之间的连接概念形式化为边和顶点特征。我们给出了两种用于挖掘确定性图行走程序的算法，这些算法按照长度递增的顺序生成程序。这些程序描述了整个图上下文中给定两个顶点集之间的线性长距离关系。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种算法用于挖掘确定性图行走程序，以描述图中两个顶点集之间的线性远距离关系。
AI.motivation: 图结构能表示复杂实体关系，但缺乏有效方法形式化并挖掘这种关系的程序表示。
AI.method: 引入图行走程序概念，设计两种算法按长度递增顺序生成确定性程序，利用边和顶点特征建模关系。
AI.result: 成功开发出可生成有序图行走程序的算法，有效表征顶点集间的长距离线性关联。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为分析图中复杂关系提供了新工具，未来可拓展至更多实际应用场景。

authors_text: Brahim Tamadazte
title: Contributions à l'asservissement visuel et à l'imagerie en médecine
title_zh: 对视觉和医学图像服务的贡献
comment: 126 pages, in French language, 100 figures, report
comments: 126 pages, in French language, 100 figures, report
abstract: This manuscript gives an overview of my research work carried out within the FEMTO-ST institute in Besançon, more particularly in the Automatic and Micro-Mechatronic Systems (AS2M) department. It is above all the result of my (co)-supervision of interns, PhD students and postdocs. I would like to pay tribute to them, for their major contribution to scientific research, here and elsewhere.
summary: This manuscript gives an overview of my research work carried out within the FEMTO-ST institute in Besançon, more particularly in the Automatic and Micro-Mechatronic Systems (AS2M) department. It is above all the result of my (co)-supervision of interns, PhD students and postdocs. I would like to pay tribute to them, for their major contribution to scientific research, here and elsewhere.
abstract_zh: 本手稿概述了我在贝桑松 FEMTO-ST 研究所进行的研究工作，特别是在自动和微机电系统 (AS2M) 部门。这首先是我对实习生、博士生和博士后（共同）监督的结果。我要向他们表示敬意，感谢他们对这里和其他地方的科学研究做出的重大贡献。

AI.tldr: 概述作者在视觉伺服与医学成像领域的研究工作，并总结其对实习生、博士生及博士后研究人员的指导成果
AI.motivation: 通过总结研究工作，表彰学生和研究人员的贡献，推动相关领域的发展
AI.method: 通过指导实习生、博士生和博士后研究人员，开展视觉伺服与医学成像领域的研究项目
AI.result: 完成了多项视觉伺服与医学成像的研究项目，并培养了多名研究人员
AI.conclusion: 强调团队合作在科研中的重要性，并感谢所有贡献者的努力

search_text: Contributions à l'asservissement visuel et à l'imagerie en médecine 对视觉和医学图像服务的贡献 This manuscript gives an overview of my research work carried out within the FEMTO-ST institute in Besançon, more particularly in the Automatic and Micro-Mechatronic Systems (AS2M) department. It is above all the result of my (co)-supervision of interns, PhD students and postdocs. I would like to pay tribute to them, for their major contribution to scientific research, here and elsewhere. 本手稿概述了我在贝桑松 FEMTO-ST 研究所进行的研究工作，特别是在自动和微机电系统 (AS2M) 部门。这首先是我对实习生、博士生和博士后（共同）监督的结果。我要向他们表示敬意，感谢他们对这里和其他地方的科学研究做出的重大贡献。 概述作者在视觉伺服与医学成像领域的研究工作，并总结其对实习生、博士生及博士后研究人员的指导成果 Brahim Tamadazte cs.RO,eess.IV,physics.med-ph

authors_text: Abhishek Mukhopadhyay,Shubham Agarwal,Patrick Dylan Zwick,Pradipta Biswas
title: To show or not to show: Redacting sensitive text from videos of electronic displays
title_zh: 显示或不显示：从电子显示屏的视频中编辑敏感文本
abstract: With the increasing prevalence of video recordings there is a growing need for tools that can maintain the privacy of those recorded. In this paper, we define an approach for redacting personally identifiable text from videos using a combination of optical character recognition (OCR) and natural language processing (NLP) techniques. We examine the relative performance of this approach when used with different OCR models, specifically Tesseract and the OCR system from Google Cloud Vision (GCV). For the proposed approach the performance of GCV, in both accuracy and speed, is significantly higher than Tesseract. Finally, we explore the advantages and disadvantages of both models in real-world applications.
summary: With the increasing prevalence of video recordings there is a growing need for tools that can maintain the privacy of those recorded. In this paper, we define an approach for redacting personally identifiable text from videos using a combination of optical character recognition (OCR) and natural language processing (NLP) techniques. We examine the relative performance of this approach when used with different OCR models, specifically Tesseract and the OCR system from Google Cloud Vision (GCV). For the proposed approach the performance of GCV, in both accuracy and speed, is significantly higher than Tesseract. Finally, we explore the advantages and disadvantages of both models in real-world applications.
abstract_zh: 随着视频录制的日益普及，对能够保护录制者隐私的工具的需求也越来越大。在本文中，我们定义了一种结合光学字符识别（OCR）和自然语言处理（NLP）技术从视频中编辑个人可识别文本的方法。我们检查了这种方法与不同 OCR 模型（特别是 Tesseract 和 Google Cloud Vision (GCV) 的 OCR 系统）一起使用时的相对性能。对于所提出的方法，GCV 在准确性和速度方面的性能都明显高于 Tesseract。最后，我们探讨了这两种模型在实际应用中的优点和缺点。

AI.tldr: 提出一种结合光学字符识别（OCR）和自然语言处理（NLP）技术的方法，从电子显示屏的视频中删除敏感文本以保护隐私。
AI.motivation: 随着视频录制技术的普及，保护被记录者隐私的需求日益增长，需要有效工具隐藏视频中的个人身份信息。
AI.method: 采用OCR技术识别视频中的文本，结合NLP处理敏感信息，并对比Tesseract与Google Cloud Vision两种OCR模型的性能差异。
AI.result: Google Cloud Vision在文本识别准确率和处理速度上显著优于Tesseract，但实际应用中需权衡两者优缺点。
AI.conclusion: GCV在敏感文本红action任务中表现更优，但实际部署时需根据场景需求综合选择OCR模型。

authors_text: Wei Su
title: An Entropy-based Measure of Intelligence Degree of System Structures
title_zh: 基于熵的系统结构智能程度度量
abstract: In this paper, we investigate how to measure the intelligence of systems under specific structures. Two indicators are adopted to characterize the intelligence of a given structure, namely the function diversity of the structure, and the ability to generate order under specific environments. A measure of intelligence degree is proposed, with which the intelligence degree of several basic structures is calculated. It is shown that some structures are indeed "smarter" than the others under the proposed measure. The results add a possible way of revealing the evolution mechanism of natural life and constructing life-like structures with high intelligence degree.
summary: In this paper, we investigate how to measure the intelligence of systems under specific structures. Two indicators are adopted to characterize the intelligence of a given structure, namely the function diversity of the structure, and the ability to generate order under specific environments. A measure of intelligence degree is proposed, with which the intelligence degree of several basic structures is calculated. It is shown that some structures are indeed "smarter" than the others under the proposed measure. The results add a possible way of revealing the evolution mechanism of natural life and constructing life-like structures with high intelligence degree.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究如何衡量特定结构下系统的智能。采用两个指标来表征给定结构的智能性，即结构的功能多样性和在特定环境下生成秩序的能力。提出了一种智能程度的度量方法，计算了几种基本结构的智能程度。结果表明，在所提议的措施下，某些结构确实比其他结构“更智能”。该成果为揭示自然生命的进化机制、构建高智能程度的类生命结构提供了一种可能的途径。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于熵的系统结构智能程度度量方法，用于评估不同结构的智能水平。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以有效量化系统结构的智能，需通过功能多样性与环境适应性指标进行更准确的评估。
AI.method: 采用功能多样性与环境生成秩序能力作为指标，结合熵理论构建智能程度度量模型。
AI.result: 计算出多种基础结构的智能程度，发现部分结构确实更智能，为理解生命进化及构建高智能结构提供新途径。
AI.conclusion: 该度量方法为揭示自然生命进化机制及构建高智能类生命结构提供了理论依据。

authors_text: Jiantao Wu,Fabrizio Orlandi,Tarek AlSkaif,Declan O'Sullivan,Soumyabrata Dev
title: A semantic web approach to uplift decentralized household energy data
title_zh: 提升分散式家庭能源数据的语义网络方法
comment: Published in Sustainable Energy, Grids and Networks (SEGAN) 2022
comments: Published in Sustainable Energy, Grids and Networks (SEGAN) 2022
abstract: In a decentralized household energy system comprised of various devices such as home appliances, electric vehicles, and solar panels, end-users are able to dig deeper into the system's details and further achieve energy sustainability if they are presented with data on the electric energy consumption and production at the granularity of the device. However, many databases in this field are siloed from other domains, including solely information pertaining to energy. This may result in the loss of information (e.g. weather) on each device's energy use. Meanwhile, a large number of these datasets have been extensively used in computational modeling techniques such as machine learning models. While such computational approaches achieve great accuracy and performance by concentrating only on a local view of datasets, model reliability cannot be guaranteed since such models are very vulnerable to data input fluctuations when information omission is taken into account. This article tackles the data isolation issue in the field of smart energy systems by examining Semantic Web methods on top of a household energy system. We offer an ontology-based approach for managing decentralized data at the device-level resolution in a system. As a consequence, the scope of the data associated with each device may easily be expanded in an interoperable manner throughout the Web, and additional information, such as weather, can be obtained from the Web, provided that the data is organized according to W3C standards.
summary: In a decentralized household energy system comprised of various devices such as home appliances, electric vehicles, and solar panels, end-users are able to dig deeper into the system's details and further achieve energy sustainability if they are presented with data on the electric energy consumption and production at the granularity of the device. However, many databases in this field are siloed from other domains, including solely information pertaining to energy. This may result in the loss of information (e.g. weather) on each device's energy use. Meanwhile, a large number of these datasets have been extensively used in computational modeling techniques such as machine learning models. While such computational approaches achieve great accuracy and performance by concentrating only on a local view of datasets, model reliability cannot be guaranteed since such models are very vulnerable to data input fluctuations when information omission is taken into account. This article tackles the data isolation issue in the field of smart energy systems by examining Semantic Web methods on top of a household energy system. We offer an ontology-based approach for managing decentralized data at the device-level resolution in a system. As a consequence, the scope of the data associated with each device may easily be expanded in an interoperable manner throughout the Web, and additional information, such as weather, can be obtained from the Web, provided that the data is organized according to W3C standards.
abstract_zh: 在由家电、电动汽车、太阳能电池板等各种设备组成的分散式家庭能源系统中，终端用户如果能够获得设备粒度的电能消耗和生产数据，就能够更深入地挖掘系统的细节，进一步实现能源的可持续性。然而，该领域的许多数据库与其他领域是孤立的，仅包括与能源有关的信息。这可能会导致每个设备的能源使用信息（例如天气）丢失。同时，大量数据集已广泛应用于机器学习模型等计算建模技术。虽然这种计算方法通过仅关注数据集的局部视图来实现很高的准确性和性能，但无法保证模型的可靠性，因为当考虑到信息遗漏时，此类模型非常容易受到数据输入波动的影响。本文通过研究家庭能源系统之上的语义网方法来解决智能能源系统领域的数据隔离问题。我们提供基于本体的方法，用于在系统中以设备级分辨率管理分散数据。因此，与每个设备相关的数据范围可以很容易地以可互操作的方式扩展到整个 Web，并且可以从 Web 获得附加信息，例如天气，只要数据是根据 W3C 标准组织的。

AI.tldr: 提出基于语义网络的方法解决家庭能源数据孤岛问题，整合多源数据提升系统可持续性。
AI.motivation: 现有家庭能源数据存在领域孤岛，导致关键信息（如天气）缺失，影响机器学习模型可靠性。
AI.method: 采用本体论方法构建设备级数据管理框架，遵循W3C标准实现数据互操作性。
AI.result: 实现设备数据的跨域扩展，整合天气等外部信息，增强系统可持续性。
AI.conclusion: 语义网络方法有效打破数据孤岛，提高数据互操作性与模型可靠性。
